summary: Soundboard capture of a developing 1967 show featuring a smoking Viola Lee Blues and a standout New Potato Caboose, recorded well despite occasional speed quirks.
review: The two‑track set showcases the band in early improvisational mode – a 20‑minute Viola Lee>Feedback jam that many reviewers describe as intense and “awesome,” plus a tight New Potato Caboose that earns high marks. The soundboard source is generally clear, though one listener notes the tape runs slightly fast, which can be corrected with audio tools.

ai_rating.rationale: Seven detailed reviews are overwhelmingly positive about both performance and recording; only minor criticisms (inconsistent playing on Viola Lee and slight speed issues) keep the rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard recording praised as “awesomely recorded”; one reviewer mentions a slightly fast tape speed that can be corrected.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief two‑song set with an extended Viola Lee>Feedback jam that captures the band’s early jamming evolution; the transition into New Potato is tight and energetic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Flashes of brilliance noted, though some feel he gets stuck at times during Viola Lee.
band_member_comments.Phil: Great bass performance highlighted by multiple reviewers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Nice vocal contribution on New Potato mentioned.
